slides.title: Combine Conditions Like a Pro
slides.body: Python's **logical operators** — `and`, `or`, and `not` — let you check multiple conditions at once. Think of them as the glue that turns simple true/false checks into smart, real-world rules.
slides.tip: These three keywords are the backbone of almost every decision your code will ever make.

slides.title: `and` — Both Doors Must Open
slides.body: `and` is `True` only when **both** sides are `True`. If either side fails, the whole thing is `False`.
slides.code:
age = 20
has_ticket = True

if age >= 18 and has_ticket:
    print("Welcome in!")
slides.tip: Think of `and` as a two-lock door — you need both keys.

slides.title: `or` Needs Just One Truth
slides.body: `or` is `True` if **at least one** side is `True`. Use it when multiple paths lead to the same outcome.
slides.code:
is_member = False
is_vip = True

if is_member or is_vip:
    print("Access granted!")
slides.tip: `or` is like having two keys — either one unlocks the door.

slides.title: `not` Flips the Value
slides.body: `not` turns `True` into `False` and `False` into `True`. Combine it with `and`/`or` to exclude conditions.
slides.code:
is_banned = False
is_vip = True

if is_vip and not is_banned:
    print("Come right in!")
slides.tip: not is_banned reads almost like plain English — that's the goal!

slides.title: Watch Out: `or` Is Not a List
slides.body: A classic beginner trap: `color == "red" or "blue"` does **not** check both colors. The string `"blue"` is always truthy, so the condition is always `True`.
slides.code:
color = "green"

# WRONG — always True!
if color == "red" or "blue":
    print("matched")

# Correct
if color == "red" or color == "blue":
    print("matched")
slides.tip: Always write the full comparison on both sides of `or`.

slides.title: You've Got the Logic!
slides.body: You now know `and` (both true), `or` (one true), and `not` (flip it). Mix them with parentheses to write clean, readable conditions for any situation.
slides.tip: When in doubt, add parentheses — they make complex conditions crystal clear.
